campus: Milan / Pavia (inter-university programme jointly organised by the University of Milano-Bicocca, the University of Pavia and the University of Milan; admission applications are managed by the University of Milan)
admission_type: open access with entry requirements examination

academic_requirements.summary: Candidates must hold (or obtain by 31 December 2026) a bachelor's degree (or equivalent three-year university degree, including a foreign degree recognised as appropriate by the University) and satisfy specific curricular requirements before the Admission Board verifies their initial preparation, possibly via an interview.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: A bachelor's degree (or equivalent three-year university diploma) in the degree classes listed on the programme 'Application' page, or a foreign university degree recognised as appropriate by the University. Graduating students may apply provided they obtain the degree no later than 31 December 2026.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements_cfu: A minimum of 30 CFU across the listed scientific-disciplinary sectors, of which at least 12 CFU in computer science / mathematics / statistics (INF/01, ING-INF/05, MAT/01, MAT/02, MAT/05, MAT/06, MAT/07, MAT/09, SECS-S/01, SECS-S/03) and at least 12 CFU in humanities, cognitive sciences and law sectors (M-FIL/02, M-FIL/03, M-FIL/05, M-PSI/01, M-PSI/02, BIO/09, IUS/01, IUS/04, IUS/08, IUS/09, IUS/10, IUS/14, IUS/20).
academic_requirements.personal_preparation: Personal preparation is assessed by the Admission Board, which may request an in-person or remote interview covering algorithms and programming, logic, probability, statistics, algebra, and basic philosophical / cognitive-science / legal foundations of AI.
academic_requirements.international_qualification: Foreign qualifications are assessed for eligibility by the University in compliance with ministerial regulations and bilateral agreements. 2 places are reserved for Marco Polo programme candidates.

language_requirements.required_level: English at level B2 or higher of the CEFR.
accepted_proofs: A valid English language certificate at level B2 or higher (issued no more than 3 years before).
A University of Milan SLAM language-centre certification (within the last 4 years).
A bachelor's degree delivered entirely or predominantly in English.
Passing the entry test administered by the University of Milan Language Centre (SLAM).
language_requirements.minimum_scores: [uncertain] Specific minimum IELTS/TOEFL/Cambridge scores are not published on the HCAI programme/enrolment page; the University of Milan SLAM recognised-certificate list applies.
language_requirements.exemption_rules: Holders of a bachelor's degree delivered entirely/predominantly in English, or of a recognised B2+ certificate, are exempt from the SLAM entry test.
language_requirements.assessment_or_upload_stage: English proficiency must be demonstrated at admission; candidates without an accepted certificate may sit the SLAM entry test (calendar at https://www.unimi.it/en/node/39267/).

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: Entry requirements assessment (individual admission interview run by the Admission Board). No standardised external test (TOLC/GRE/GMAT).
entry_exam_or_test.mandatory: An interview may be requested; the Admission Board may admit candidates without further interview where curricular and language requirements are clearly met.
entry_exam_or_test.is_selective: Open with entry requirements examination - the interview verifies adequacy of preparation rather than ranking candidates.
entry_exam_or_test.format: In-person or remote interview covering algorithms and programming, logic, probability, statistics, algebra, and basic philosophical / cognitive / legal foundations of AI.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: [uncertain] No numerical pass threshold is published; the Admission Board communicates the assessment outcome by email.

rounds: A.Y. 2026/2027: online admission application for EU candidates and equivalents open from 22 January 2026 to 30 October 2026 (via studente.unimi.it).
application_deadline_eu.degree_completion_deadline: Degree and entry requirements must be obtained by 31 December 2026.
application_deadline_eu.enrolment_deadline: Enrolment of admitted students from 5 May 2026 to 15 January 2027.
application_deadline_eu.notes: Applications and enrolment are managed by the University of Milan because the programme is administered there; a EUR 30 application fee is due by the admission deadline.

rounds: A.Y. 2026/2027: non-EU candidates applying for a visa must submit the online admission application from 22 January 2026 to 30 April 2026 (via studente.unimi.it).
application_deadline_non_eu.visa_or_pre_enrolment_deadline: Non-EU visa candidates must submit the Universitaly pre-enrolment application by 31 July 2026; applications after this date are not accepted.
application_deadline_non_eu.foreign_qualification_documents_deadline: Documentation for candidates with foreign qualifications (legalised/apostilled degree, Statement of Comparability or Declaration of Value, etc.) must be completed by 30 November 2026, otherwise enrolment is cancelled.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: Candidates with qualifications obtained abroad apply via apply.unimi.it; language requirements for non-EU visa candidates follow ministerial provisions for 2026/27 published on the dedicated webpage.

required_documents.document_name: Valid ID document (identity card or passport)
required_documents.notes: Uploaded in .jpg, .pdf or .png; passport (or other valid ID) is mandatory for candidates with foreign qualifications applying via apply.unimi.it.

required_documents.document_name: Self-certification of degree/enrolment with exam records (Italian qualifications) or Bachelor's degree certificate (foreign qualifications)
required_documents.notes: For Italian degrees: self-certification with exams, grades, ECTS credits and SSD sectors. For foreign degrees: B.Sc. or degree-awarding certificate in Italian/English/French/German/Spanish.

required_documents.document_name: Diploma Supplement or transcript of records (courses, marks, ECTS, academic field, grading system)
required_documents.notes: Required for candidates with foreign qualifications; in English, Italian, French, German or Spanish.

required_documents.document_name: English language proficiency proof (B2+ certificate, SLAM certification, English-taught degree, or SLAM entry test)
required_documents.notes: B2-or-higher CEFR; certificate must be issued within the last 3 years.

required_documents.document_name: Statement of Comparability (ENIC-NARIC / CIMEA) or Declaration of Value, plus legalised/apostilled degree, high-school diploma, Italian tax code, residence-permit receipt
required_documents.notes: Required to finalise enrolment for foreign-qualification candidates; for Lisbon Convention signatory countries the Diploma Supplement or ARDI Correspondence Statement may replace the DoV/Statement of Comparability. Due by 30 November 2026.
Payable by the admission deadline, on pain of exclusion.

source_quotes.quote: Teaching Language: English; Campuses: Milano/Pavia; Degree Class: LM-55; Inter-university course jointly organized by University of Milano-Bicocca, University of Pavia, and University of Milan.
Applications for admission are managed by the University of Milan.

Online admission application for EU candidates and equivalents from 22 January to 30 October 2026; Online admission application for non EU candidates applying for a visa from 22 January to 30 April 2026; Enrolment of admitted students from 5 May 2026 to 15 January 2027.
All students falling under this category are required to submit an application for pre-enrolment through Universitaly platform by 31 July 2026.

Minimum 30 CFUs: at least 12 CFU in computer science / mathematics / statistics and at least 12 CFU in humanities, cognitive sciences and law; Proficiency in English at level B2 or higher according to the Common European Framework.

uncertainty_notes: Teaching language confirmed fully English on the official en.unimib.it programme page (also on hcai.cdl.unimi.it).
CFU/subject prerequisites confirmed: 30 CFU total (>=12 CFU computer science/maths/statistics, >=12 CFU humanities/cognitive sciences/law) with the listed SSD codes.

B2 English confirmed (B2 or higher CEFR); however specific minimum IELTS/TOEFL/Cambridge scores are not published on the HCAI page - the University of Milan SLAM recognised-certificate list applies, so language_requirements minimum_scores is marked uncertain.

EU and non-EU calls confirmed from the official 2026/27 admission notice (LM-55 R). The programme is inter-university but administered by the University of Milan, so the official call PDF and the fees link are hosted on unimi.it rather than on a unimib.it subdomain; included per task scope.

Deadlines re-verified 2026-06-11: this programme is administered by the University of Milan (lead university) via studente.unimi.it, NOT by apply.unimib.it. The UNIMIB unified Master 2026/27 calendar therefore does NOT apply. Existing programme-specific deadlines (EU 22 Jan - 30 Oct 2026; non-EU visa 22 Jan - 30 Apr 2026) preserved.
